title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for complexity, timing, profiling, memory, and capacity and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for correctness-preserving capacity study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.
Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

options.text: Run the Performance Regression boundary case for correctness-preserving capacity study, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Performance Regression result because visible correctness-preserving capacity study output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Performance Regression.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Performance Regression for correctness-preserving capacity study.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Regression
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Performance Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 5155 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Regression.
steps.why: Retrieving Performance Regression through the concrete correctness-preserving capacity study case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Regression, keep Performance Regression connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.
